articles.headline: Government Launches National Urban Resilience Mission 2.0 to Bolster Climate-Resilient Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveils the second phase of the National Urban Resilience Mission.
• Focuses on integrating climate adaptation strategies into urban planning and development across Indian cities.
• Aims to enhance disaster preparedness, green infrastructure, and sustainable resource management in urban areas.
• Emphasizes collaboration between central, state governments, urban local bodies, and private sector stakeholders.
• Builds upon the successes and lessons learned from the initial phase, addressing emerging climate challenges.
prelimsPoints: National Urban Resilience Mission 2.0 launched by Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Objectives include climate adaptation, disaster preparedness, green infrastructure in urban areas.
Focus on multi-stakeholder collaboration for implementation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenges of urbanization and climate change in India and the need for such missions.
Analyze the role of integrated urban planning in achieving sustainable development goals.
Evaluate the potential impact of the mission on local governance and citizen participation in urban resilience efforts.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a modern, green, and resilient city with smart infrastructure.
articles.trivia: India is projected to have one of the largest urban populations in the world by 2050, making urban resilience crucial.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Privacy in Landmark Ruling on Digital Healthcare Data
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivers a significant judgment emphasizing data privacy in the context of digital healthcare records.
• The ruling mandates robust data protection frameworks and stringent consent mechanisms for all digital health platforms and providers.
• Reaffirms the principles established in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017), extending them to sensitive personal health information.
• Directs the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law addressing specific concerns in the health sector.
• Highlights the balance between leveraging technology for public health and safeguarding individual privacy and autonomy.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on digital healthcare data privacy.
Reaffirms principles of Puttaswamy judgment (2017) on Right to Privacy.
Mandates robust data protection and consent mechanisms for digital health platforms.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the judgment on the implementation of India's digital health ecosystem, such as the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
Discuss the challenges in balancing innovation in healthcare technology with the protection of fundamental rights like privacy.
Evaluate the need for a specific legal framework for health data protection in India, drawing comparisons with international standards.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image representing data security and digital privacy, with a symbol of justice.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment unanimously affirmed that the Right to Privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21 of the Constitution.

articles.headline: India-EFTA Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Stages, Promising Boost for Key Sectors
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Free Trade Association (EFTA) Trade and Economic Partnership Agreement (TEPA) are nearing conclusion.
• The agreement is expected to significantly boost market access for Indian pharmaceutical products, IT services, and textiles.
• EFTA comprises four non-EU European states: Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein.
• India seeks greater investment and technology transfer from EFTA countries, especially in advanced manufacturing and green technologies.
• The deal aims to enhance bilateral trade and economic cooperation, opening new avenues for Indian exports.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: India-EFTA TEPA negotiations in final stages.
EFTA members: Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein.
Key Indian sectors to benefit: Pharma, IT, Textiles.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the India-EFTA FTA for India's trade diversification efforts and economic growth.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for Indian industries, particularly the pharmaceutical and IT sectors, from this agreement.
Evaluate how such bilateral trade agreements align with India's broader foreign policy and 'Make in India' initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and EFTA member countries symbolizing trade relations.
articles.trivia: EFTA was established in 1960 as an alternative to the European Economic Community (EEC), now the European Union.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements

articles.headline: ISRO Achieves Milestone with Successful Test of Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Landing Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducts a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
• The test involved precise autonomous landing maneuvers, simulating a space return trajectory at a designated runway.
• This achievement is a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aiming to drastically reduce space launch costs.
• The RLV technology is critical for India's future space exploration missions, including Gaganyaan and planned lunar/interplanetary probes.
• The successful demonstration enhances India's capabilities in advanced aerospace engineering and sustainable space access.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tests landing technology for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV).
Aims to reduce space launch costs and enhance mission frequency.
Crucial for future missions like Gaganyaan.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's ambitions in the global space sector.
Evaluate how indigenous development of such advanced technologies contributes to national self-reliance and economic benefits.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for ISRO in competing with global players in the commercial space launch market.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle making a precise landing on a runway.
articles.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is designed to test various aspects of reusable launch vehicle technology, including hypersonic flight and autonomous landing.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.area: Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life

articles.headline: India's Tiger Population Registers Further Growth in Latest Census, Conservation Efforts Lauded
articles.explainer:
• The latest All India Tiger Estimation Report reveals a continued increase in India's tiger population, reinforcing its status as a global leader in tiger conservation.
• The census, conducted using advanced camera trapping and M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) technology, shows healthy growth in various tiger reserves.
• The success is attributed to sustained efforts under Project Tiger, habitat restoration, and anti-poaching measures.
• Challenges remain, including habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and the need for corridor protection.
• The report underscores the importance of community involvement and scientific monitoring for long-term conservation success.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Latest All India Tiger Estimation Report shows growth in tiger population.
Census uses camera trapping and M-STrIPES technology.
Success attributed to Project Tiger and conservation measures.
India accounts for over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted approach adopted by India for tiger conservation and its global significance.
Examine the persistent challenges in tiger conservation, such as habitat loss, poaching, and human-wildlife conflict, and suggest sustainable solutions.
Evaluate the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in successful wildlife conservation programs in India.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger captured in its natural habitat, symbolizing successful conservation.
articles.trivia: India conducts a tiger census every four years, making it one of the largest wildlife surveys in the world.

articles.headline: India Chairs BIMSTEC Foreign Ministers' Meeting, Emphasizing Regional Connectivity and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India hosts the annual Foreign Ministers' Meeting of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC).
• The agenda focuses on enhancing regional connectivity, trade, investment, and energy cooperation among member states.
• Discussions also cover counter-terrorism, disaster management, and cultural exchanges to foster greater people-to-people contact.
• BIMSTEC members include Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand, representing a crucial bridge between South and Southeast Asia.
• India reiterates its commitment to strengthening the organization for a prosperous and secure Bay of Bengal region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India hosts BIMSTEC Foreign Ministers' Meeting.
BIMSTEC members: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Thailand.
Focus on regional connectivity, trade, and security cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of BIMSTEC for India's 'Act East Policy' and its role in fostering regional stability and growth.
Compare and contrast BIMSTEC's potential and challenges with other regional groupings like SAARC.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation within BIMSTEC and their importance for India's economic and security interests.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member countries arranged in a circle, symbolizing unity and cooperation.
articles.trivia: BIMSTEC was established in 1997 with the Bangkok Declaration, initially with four members (BIST-EC).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements

articles.headline: National Health Mission Expands Telemedicine Services, Bridging Healthcare Gap in Remote Tribal Regions
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Mission (NHM) announces a significant expansion of its telemedicine services, specifically targeting remote and underserved tribal areas.
• The initiative aims to provide accessible and specialized medical consultations to tribal communities, leveraging digital health infrastructure.
• It integrates with the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to ensure seamless record-keeping and continuity of care.
• Training programs for community health workers and local medical staff are being intensified to facilitate last-mile delivery of services.
• This expansion seeks to address health disparities and improve health outcomes among vulnerable tribal populations.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: NHM expands telemedicine services to remote tribal areas.
Integrates with Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
Aims to improve healthcare access and reduce disparities for tribal communities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by tribal communities in accessing healthcare and how telemedicine can act as a transformative solution.
Evaluate the role of technology and digital infrastructure in achieving universal health coverage in India.
Examine the ethical considerations and implementation hurdles associated with digital health initiatives in diverse cultural contexts.
articles.imageDescription: A tribal woman consulting a doctor via a telemedicine screen in a remote village setting.
articles.trivia: The National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013, subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.area: Government Policies and Interventions for Development

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic Tunnel in Eastern Ladakh, Enhancing All-Weather Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) successfully completes a strategically vital tunnel in Eastern Ladakh, significantly boosting connectivity in the region.
• The all-weather tunnel will provide year-round access to critical border areas, which are often cut off during winter months due to heavy snowfall.
• This infrastructure development is crucial for military logistics, facilitating faster troop and equipment movement along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• It also benefits local civilian populations by improving access to essential services and promoting economic activities.
• The project underscores India's accelerated efforts in border infrastructure development to enhance national security and regional development.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: BRO completes strategic tunnel in Eastern Ladakh.
Provides all-weather connectivity to critical border areas.
Enhances military logistics and benefits local civilians.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of border infrastructure development in challenging terrains like Eastern Ladakh for India's national security.
Examine the role of organizations like BRO in enhancing strategic capabilities and promoting socio-economic development in border regions.
Analyze the geopolitical implications of accelerated infrastructure development along India's northern borders.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed tunnel entrance in a mountainous, snowy landscape of Ladakh.
articles.trivia: The BRO is responsible for constructing and maintaining road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.area: Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Clears New Policy to Boost Exploration and Mining of Critical Minerals
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approves a new comprehensive policy aimed at accelerating the exploration and mining of critical minerals in India.
• The policy seeks to reduce India's import dependence for essential minerals like Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Rare Earth Elements (REEs).
• It encourages private sector participation, foreign investment, and advanced exploration technologies through streamlined regulatory processes.
• Critical minerals are vital for India's energy transition, electric vehicle manufacturing, digital economy, and defence industries.
• The initiative aligns with India's goal of achieving strategic autonomy and securing raw material supply chains for future technologies.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Union Cabinet approves new policy for critical mineral exploration and mining.
Aims to reduce import dependence for minerals like Lithium, Cobalt, REEs.
Encourages private and foreign investment in the sector.
Critical for energy transition, EVs, digital economy, defence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth, energy security, and technological advancement.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with domestic critical mineral exploration and mining, including environmental concerns and technological gaps.
Evaluate how the new policy framework can contribute to India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision and strengthen its position in global supply chains.
articles.imageDescription: Various critical minerals displayed, with symbols of green energy and electronics in the background.
articles.trivia: India currently relies heavily on imports for most of its critical mineral requirements, with China being a dominant supplier globally.

articles.headline: Recent Excavations at Ancient Harappan Site in Gujarat Uncover New Insights into Indus Valley Civilization
articles.explainer:
• Ongoing archaeological excavations at a newly identified Harappan site in Gujarat yield significant discoveries.
• Researchers uncover unique artifacts, structural remains, and evidence of advanced urban planning, shedding new light on the Indus Valley Civilization.
• The findings suggest previously unknown trade routes and cultural interactions within the Harappan network.
• The site provides crucial data for understanding the socio-economic structure and the eventual decline of the civilization.
• This discovery further solidifies Gujarat's importance as a hub of Harappan settlements, complementing sites like Dholavira and Lothal.
gsSubTopics: History of India and Indian National Movement
prelimsPoints: New Harappan site discovered/excavated in Gujarat.
Findings include unique artifacts and structural remains.
Provides new insights into trade routes and urban planning of Indus Valley Civilization.
Gujarat is home to other major Harappan sites like Dholavira and Lothal.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries in reshaping our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization's spread, trade, and cultural characteristics.
Examine the methods used in modern archaeological excavations and their role in historical reconstruction.
Evaluate the challenges in preserving and managing ancient heritage sites in India.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating an ancient site, revealing pottery and structural ruins.
articles.trivia: Dholavira in Gujarat is one of the five largest Harappan sites and is known for its unique water management system and multi-layered fortifications.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician and Former MP Suravaram Sudhakar Reddy Passes Away at 83
articles.explainer:
• Suravaram Sudhakar Reddy, a prominent Indian politician and former Member of Parliament, passed away at the age of 83.
• He served as an MP from the Nalgonda constituency in Andhra Pradesh (now Telangana) for two terms (1998–1999, 2004–2009).
• A veteran leader of the Communist Party of India (CPI), he was known for his staunch advocacy for farmers' rights and social justice.
• Reddy also held the position of General Secretary of the Communist Party of India, playing a significant role in national politics.
• His political career spanned several decades, contributing to parliamentary debates and public life.
prelimsPoints: Suravaram Sudhakar Reddy, former MP and CPI leader, passed away.
Served as MP from Nalgonda constituency (1998-1999, 2004-2009).
Former General Secretary of CPI.
articles.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of Suravaram Sudhakar Reddy.
articles.trivia: The Communist Party of India (CPI) is one of the oldest political parties in India, founded in 1925.

articles.headline: Kerala MLA Vazhoor Soman Dies at 72
articles.explainer:
• Vazhoor Soman, a sitting Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Kerala, passed away at the age of 72.
• He represented the Puttupally constituency in the Kerala Legislative Assembly since 2021.
• Soman was a seasoned politician known for his dedication to local development and public service.
• His demise creates a vacancy in the state assembly, necessitating a by-election.
• He was a respected figure in Kerala politics, contributing to various legislative and social initiatives.
prelimsPoints: Vazhoor Soman, sitting MLA from Puttupally constituency in Kerala, passed away.
His demise necessitates a by-election for the constituency.
articles.trivia: A by-election is held to fill a vacancy arising during the term of a legislative body due to resignation, death, or disqualification.

articles.headline: Renowned Punjabi Actor and Comedian Jaswinder Bhalla Passes Away at 65
articles.explainer:
• Jaswinder Bhalla, a celebrated Punjabi actor and comedian, passed away at the age of 65.
• He was widely known for his iconic roles in numerous Punjabi films such as 'Jatt & Juliet', 'Carry On Jatta', and 'Mahaul Theek Hai'.
• Bhalla's unique comedic timing, satirical performances, and memorable dialogues made him a household name in Punjab and among the Punjabi diaspora.
• He also had a distinguished career in television and theatre, contributing significantly to Punjabi popular culture.
• His demise marks the end of an era for Punjabi comedy and cinema.
articles.trivia: Jaswinder Bhalla was also a professor of agriculture at Punjab Agricultural University before pursuing acting full-time.

articles.headline: Former Member of Parliament Kali Prasad Pandey Dies at 78
articles.explainer:
• Kali Prasad Pandey, a former Member of Parliament, passed away at the age of 78.
• He served as an MP in the 8th Lok Sabha (1984–1989), representing a constituency in Uttar Pradesh.
• Pandey was known for his active participation in parliamentary proceedings and commitment to public service.
• His political career involved addressing various social and economic issues relevant to his constituents.
• He leaves behind a legacy of dedicated public life.
prelimsPoints: Kali Prasad Pandey, former MP (8th Lok Sabha), passed away.
Served from 1984-1989.
articles.imageDescription: Formal photo of Kali Prasad Pandey.
articles.trivia: The 8th Lok Sabha was constituted after the 1984 general election, which saw a landslide victory for the Indian National Congress.

articles.headline: Kannada Actor and Art Director Dinesh Mangaluru Passes Away at 55
articles.explainer:
• Dinesh Mangaluru, a versatile Kannada actor and art director, passed away at the age of 55.
• He was recognized for his impactful performances in Kannada cinema, including films like 'Smile', 'Ambari', and 'Savari'.
• Mangaluru also contributed significantly behind the scenes as a talented art director.
• His work enriched the visual aesthetics of many films, earning him respect within the industry.
• His untimely demise is a loss to the vibrant Kannada film industry.
articles.trivia: The Kannada film industry is primarily based in Bengaluru, Karnataka, and is known for its diverse range of films.

articles.headline: Actor-Politician Joy Banerjee Passes Away at 62
articles.explainer:
• Joy Banerjee, known for his dual career as an actor and politician, passed away at the age of 62.
• He had a notable presence in Bengali cinema before transitioning into active politics.
• Banerjee was associated with a prominent national political party, contesting elections and engaging in public discourse.
• His ability to connect with audiences both on screen and in political rallies made him a recognizable figure.
• His passing is mourned by both the entertainment and political communities.
articles.trivia: Many personalities from the Indian film industry have successfully transitioned into politics, leveraging their public appeal.

highlights: Government launches National Urban Resilience Mission 2.0 to enhance climate-resilient urban infrastructure and disaster preparedness.
Supreme Court reinforces individual right to privacy in digital healthcare records, mandating robust data protection frameworks.
India-EFTA Free Trade Agreement negotiations enter final stages, promising a significant boost for Indian pharmaceutical and IT sectors.
ISRO achieves a major milestone with the successful test of next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle landing technology.
Latest All India Tiger Estimation Report reveals continued growth in India's tiger population, lauding national conservation efforts.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'National Urban Resilience Mission 2.0':
1. It is launched by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
2. Its primary objective is to integrate climate adaptation strategies into urban planning.
3. It focuses solely on metropolitan cities to enhance disaster preparedness.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The National Urban Resilience Mission 2.0 is launched by the Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs. Statement 2 is correct, as its primary focus is on integrating climate adaptation into urban planning. Statement 3 is incorrect as it aims to cover Indian cities broadly, not solely metropolitan ones, and emphasizes collaboration across various levels of government and stakeholders.

prelimsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court ruling reinforcing the right to privacy in digital healthcare data explicitly reaffirms the principles established in which landmark judgment?
options: (a) Kesavananda Bharati vs State of Kerala (1973)
(b) Maneka Gandhi vs Union of India (1978)
(c) Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) vs Union of India (2017)
(d) S.R. Bommai vs Union of India (1994)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) vs Union of India (2017)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's ruling explicitly reaffirms the principles of the Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) vs Union of India judgment of 2017, which declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following countries are members of the European Free Trade Association (EFTA) with whom India is finalizing a Free Trade Agreement?
prelimsMocks.explanation: EFTA comprises four non-EU European states: Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein. These are the countries with whom India is negotiating the Trade and Economic Partnership Agreement (TEPA).

prelimsMocks.question: The M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) technology is primarily used in India for:
options: (a) Tracking illegal mining activities in forest areas
(b) Monitoring the health and population of migratory birds
(c) Conducting the All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census)
(d) Assessing the impact of climate change on Himalayan glaciers
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Conducting the All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census)
prelimsMocks.explanation: M-STrIPES is an electronic monitoring system developed for tiger reserves in India. It is used to strengthen protection and ecological monitoring of tigers, playing a crucial role in the All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census).

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Critical Minerals Policy:
1. The new policy aims to increase India's import dependence for minerals like Lithium and Cobalt.
2. It encourages private sector participation and foreign investment in exploration and mining.
3. Critical minerals are essential for India's energy transition and electric vehicle manufacturing.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the new policy aims to *reduce* India's import dependence for critical minerals. Statement 2 is correct as it encourages private and foreign investment. Statement 3 is correct, as critical minerals are vital for green energy, EVs, and high-tech industries.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the critical balance between rapid urbanization and environmental sustainability in India. In light of initiatives like the National Urban Resilience Mission, critically evaluate the effectiveness of government policies in fostering climate-resilient cities.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation. GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Disaster and disaster management.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the challenge of urbanization vs. environment. Explain the objectives of National Urban Resilience Mission. Analyze its potential effectiveness by discussing its strengths (integrated approach, multi-stakeholder) and weaknesses (implementation challenges, funding, land-use planning). Conclude with recommendations for a holistic approach.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's ruling on digital healthcare data privacy underscores the tension between individual fundamental rights and public health objectives. Examine the implications of this judgment for both patients' autonomy and the future of digital health initiatives in India.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Fundamental Rights; Judiciary; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation (Health); Role of civil services in a democracy.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the core of the SC judgment and its basis in the Right to Privacy. Discuss the positive implications for patient autonomy, data security, and trust in digital health. Then, analyze the potential challenges for public health initiatives, data aggregation for research, and disease surveillance. Conclude by suggesting ways to balance these competing interests through robust legal frameworks and ethical guidelines.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for self-reliance in critical minerals through a new policy is crucial for its energy transition and strategic autonomy. Evaluate the potential economic, environmental, and geopolitical impacts of this policy, highlighting the challenges in its implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Infrastructure (Energy); Science and Technology; Security challenges and their management in border areas.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the concept of critical minerals and their importance for India. Discuss the economic benefits (reduced import bill, job creation, industrial growth) and strategic advantages (energy security, defence capabilities). Address environmental concerns related to mining and the geopolitical implications of securing supply chains. Conclude by outlining implementation challenges such as technology, investment, and regulatory hurdles, along with potential solutions.

quickBytes.text: The National Urban Resilience Mission 2.0 aims to integrate climate adaptation into urban planning.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy a fundamental right under Article 21.
EFTA comprises Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein, non-EU European states.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program seeks to reduce space access costs significantly.
Project Tiger, launched in 1973, is a major wildlife conservation initiative in India.
BIMSTEC is a regional organization comprising seven member states in the Bay of Bengal region.
Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission aims to create a national digital health ecosystem in India.
Border Roads Organisation (BRO) constructs and maintains road networks in India's border areas.
Critical minerals like Lithium, Cobalt, and REEs are vital for green energy and high-tech manufacturing.
Dholavira, a Harappan city in Gujarat, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its water management.

• Researchers uncover unique artifacts, structural remains, and evidence of advanced urban planning, shedding new light on the Indus Valley Civilization.
• The findings suggest previously unknown trade routes and cultural interactions within the Harappan network.
• The site provides crucial data for understanding the socio-economic structure and the eventual decline of the civilization.
• This discovery further solidifies Gujarat's importance as a hub of Harappan settlements, complementing sites like Dholavira and Lothal.

• Bhalla's unique comedic timing, satirical performances, and memorable dialogues made him a household name in Punjab and among the Punjabi diaspora.
• He also had a distinguished career in television and theatre, contributing significantly to Punjabi popular culture.
• His demise marks the end of an era for Punjabi comedy and cinema.

• Dinesh Mangaluru, a versatile Kannada actor and art director, passed away at the age of 55.
• He was recognized for his impactful performances in Kannada cinema, including films like 'Smile', 'Ambari', and 'Savari'.
• Mangaluru also contributed significantly behind the scenes as a talented art director.
• His work enriched the visual aesthetics of many films, earning him respect within the industry.
• His untimely demise is a loss to the vibrant Kannada film industry.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Privacy in Landmark Ruling on Digital Healthcare Data
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivers a significant judgment emphasizing data privacy in the context of digital healthcare records.
• The ruling mandates robust data protection frameworks and stringent consent mechanisms for all digital health platforms and providers.
• Reaffirms the principles established in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017), extending them to sensitive personal health information.
• Directs the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law addressing specific concerns in the health sector.
• Highlights the balance between leveraging technology for public health and safeguarding individual privacy and autonomy.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on digital healthcare data privacy.
Reaffirms principles of Puttaswamy judgment (2017) on Right to Privacy.
Mandates robust data protection and consent mechanisms for digital health platforms.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the judgment on the implementation of India's digital health ecosystem, such as the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
Discuss the challenges in balancing innovation in healthcare technology with the protection of fundamental rights like privacy.
Evaluate the need for a specific legal framework for health data protection in India, drawing comparisons with international standards.
GS2.imageDescription: Abstract image representing data security and digital privacy, with a symbol of justice.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment unanimously affirmed that the Right to Privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21 of the Constitution.

GS2.headline: India Chairs BIMSTEC Foreign Ministers' Meeting, Emphasizing Regional Connectivity and Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India hosts the annual Foreign Ministers' Meeting of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC).
• The agenda focuses on enhancing regional connectivity, trade, investment, and energy cooperation among member states.
• Discussions also cover counter-terrorism, disaster management, and cultural exchanges to foster greater people-to-people contact.
• BIMSTEC members include Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand, representing a crucial bridge between South and Southeast Asia.
• India reiterates its commitment to strengthening the organization for a prosperous and secure Bay of Bengal region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India hosts BIMSTEC Foreign Ministers' Meeting.
BIMSTEC members: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Thailand.
Focus on regional connectivity, trade, and security cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of BIMSTEC for India's 'Act East Policy' and its role in fostering regional stability and growth.
Compare and contrast BIMSTEC's potential and challenges with other regional groupings like SAARC.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation within BIMSTEC and their importance for India's economic and security interests.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member countries arranged in a circle, symbolizing unity and cooperation.
GS2.trivia: BIMSTEC was established in 1997 with the Bangkok Declaration, initially with four members (BIST-EC).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements

GS2.headline: National Health Mission Expands Telemedicine Services, Bridging Healthcare Gap in Remote Tribal Regions
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Mission (NHM) announces a significant expansion of its telemedicine services, specifically targeting remote and underserved tribal areas.
• The initiative aims to provide accessible and specialized medical consultations to tribal communities, leveraging digital health infrastructure.
• It integrates with the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to ensure seamless record-keeping and continuity of care.
• Training programs for community health workers and local medical staff are being intensified to facilitate last-mile delivery of services.
• This expansion seeks to address health disparities and improve health outcomes among vulnerable tribal populations.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: NHM expands telemedicine services to remote tribal areas.
Integrates with Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
Aims to improve healthcare access and reduce disparities for tribal communities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by tribal communities in accessing healthcare and how telemedicine can act as a transformative solution.
Evaluate the role of technology and digital infrastructure in achieving universal health coverage in India.
Examine the ethical considerations and implementation hurdles associated with digital health initiatives in diverse cultural contexts.
GS2.imageDescription: A tribal woman consulting a doctor via a telemedicine screen in a remote village setting.
GS2.trivia: The National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013, subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.area: Government Policies and Interventions for Development

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician and Former MP Suravaram Sudhakar Reddy Passes Away at 83
GS2.explainer:
• Suravaram Sudhakar Reddy, a prominent Indian politician and former Member of Parliament, passed away at the age of 83.
• He served as an MP from the Nalgonda constituency in Andhra Pradesh (now Telangana) for two terms (1998–1999, 2004–2009).
• A veteran leader of the Communist Party of India (CPI), he was known for his staunch advocacy for farmers' rights and social justice.
• Reddy also held the position of General Secretary of the Communist Party of India, playing a significant role in national politics.
• His political career spanned several decades, contributing to parliamentary debates and public life.
prelimsPoints: Suravaram Sudhakar Reddy, former MP and CPI leader, passed away.
Served as MP from Nalgonda constituency (1998-1999, 2004-2009).
Former General Secretary of CPI.
GS2.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of Suravaram Sudhakar Reddy.
GS2.trivia: The Communist Party of India (CPI) is one of the oldest political parties in India, founded in 1925.

GS2.headline: Kerala MLA Vazhoor Soman Dies at 72
GS2.explainer:
• Vazhoor Soman, a sitting Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Kerala, passed away at the age of 72.
• He represented the Puttupally constituency in the Kerala Legislative Assembly since 2021.
• Soman was a seasoned politician known for his dedication to local development and public service.
• His demise creates a vacancy in the state assembly, necessitating a by-election.
• He was a respected figure in Kerala politics, contributing to various legislative and social initiatives.
prelimsPoints: Vazhoor Soman, sitting MLA from Puttupally constituency in Kerala, passed away.
His demise necessitates a by-election for the constituency.
GS2.trivia: A by-election is held to fill a vacancy arising during the term of a legislative body due to resignation, death, or disqualification.

GS2.headline: Former Member of Parliament Kali Prasad Pandey Dies at 78
GS2.explainer:
• Kali Prasad Pandey, a former Member of Parliament, passed away at the age of 78.
• He served as an MP in the 8th Lok Sabha (1984–1989), representing a constituency in Uttar Pradesh.
• Pandey was known for his active participation in parliamentary proceedings and commitment to public service.
• His political career involved addressing various social and economic issues relevant to his constituents.
• He leaves behind a legacy of dedicated public life.
prelimsPoints: Kali Prasad Pandey, former MP (8th Lok Sabha), passed away.
Served from 1984-1989.
GS2.imageDescription: Formal photo of Kali Prasad Pandey.
GS2.trivia: The 8th Lok Sabha was constituted after the 1984 general election, which saw a landslide victory for the Indian National Congress.

GS3.headline: ISRO Achieves Milestone with Successful Test of Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Landing Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducts a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
• The test involved precise autonomous landing maneuvers, simulating a space return trajectory at a designated runway.
• This achievement is a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aiming to drastically reduce space launch costs.
• The RLV technology is critical for India's future space exploration missions, including Gaganyaan and planned lunar/interplanetary probes.
• The successful demonstration enhances India's capabilities in advanced aerospace engineering and sustainable space access.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tests landing technology for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV).
Aims to reduce space launch costs and enhance mission frequency.
Crucial for future missions like Gaganyaan.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's ambitions in the global space sector.
Evaluate how indigenous development of such advanced technologies contributes to national self-reliance and economic benefits.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for ISRO in competing with global players in the commercial space launch market.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle making a precise landing on a runway.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is designed to test various aspects of reusable launch vehicle technology, including hypersonic flight and autonomous landing.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.area: Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life

GS3.headline: India's Tiger Population Registers Further Growth in Latest Census, Conservation Efforts Lauded
GS3.explainer:
• The latest All India Tiger Estimation Report reveals a continued increase in India's tiger population, reinforcing its status as a global leader in tiger conservation.
• The census, conducted using advanced camera trapping and M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) technology, shows healthy growth in various tiger reserves.
• The success is attributed to sustained efforts under Project Tiger, habitat restoration, and anti-poaching measures.
• Challenges remain, including habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and the need for corridor protection.
• The report underscores the importance of community involvement and scientific monitoring for long-term conservation success.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Latest All India Tiger Estimation Report shows growth in tiger population.
Census uses camera trapping and M-STrIPES technology.
Success attributed to Project Tiger and conservation measures.
India accounts for over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted approach adopted by India for tiger conservation and its global significance.
Examine the persistent challenges in tiger conservation, such as habitat loss, poaching, and human-wildlife conflict, and suggest sustainable solutions.
Evaluate the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in successful wildlife conservation programs in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger captured in its natural habitat, symbolizing successful conservation.
GS3.trivia: India conducts a tiger census every four years, making it one of the largest wildlife surveys in the world.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic Tunnel in Eastern Ladakh, Enhancing All-Weather Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) successfully completes a strategically vital tunnel in Eastern Ladakh, significantly boosting connectivity in the region.
• The all-weather tunnel will provide year-round access to critical border areas, which are often cut off during winter months due to heavy snowfall.
• This infrastructure development is crucial for military logistics, facilitating faster troop and equipment movement along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• It also benefits local civilian populations by improving access to essential services and promoting economic activities.
• The project underscores India's accelerated efforts in border infrastructure development to enhance national security and regional development.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: BRO completes strategic tunnel in Eastern Ladakh.
Provides all-weather connectivity to critical border areas.
Enhances military logistics and benefits local civilians.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of border infrastructure development in challenging terrains like Eastern Ladakh for India's national security.
Examine the role of organizations like BRO in enhancing strategic capabilities and promoting socio-economic development in border regions.
Analyze the geopolitical implications of accelerated infrastructure development along India's northern borders.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed tunnel entrance in a mountainous, snowy landscape of Ladakh.
GS3.trivia: The BRO is responsible for constructing and maintaining road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.area: Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
